description_long: This is a futuristic, science-fiction inspired airplane with a sleek, elongated fuselage and a forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit featuring a transparent canopy. The wings are straight and extend laterally from the midsection, with visible weaponry or equipment mounted beneath them. The airplane is equipped with two large, cylindrical jet engines mounted above the wings, giving it a distinctive, aggressive appearance. The tail section features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers. The exterior is primarily white with red accents and weathered markings, suggesting heavy use or combat readiness. The overall design is reminiscent of advanced fighter aircraft seen in popular media.
description: A sleek, sci-fi airplane with a forward cockpit, mid-mounted straight wings, twin top-mounted jet engines, and a conventional tail, finished in white and red with weathered details.
description_view_0: Front view showing the pointed nose, forward cockpit canopy, and symmetrical mid-mounted wings with visible engine intakes.
description_view_1: Top-down view highlighting the full wingspan, twin engines, and detailed surface markings along the fuselage and wings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the twin jet exhausts, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the trailing edges of the wings.
description_view_3: Bottom view revealing the underwing equipment or weaponry, landing gear housings, and the underside color pattern.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Mid-wing configuration with straight wings, no visible winglets, and slight dihedral angle; underwing pylons for equipment or weaponry.
attribute_engine_type: Twin jet engines mounted above the wings, cylindrical nacelles with visible intakes and exhausts.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Retractable tricycle landing gear with two main units and a single nose gear, partially visible in the images.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with a transparent canopy, likely single-seat based on size and design.
